info.title: AEM Admin API
info.description: AEM Admin API is used to manage the lifecycle of content and code.

tags.name: authentication
tags.description:
The API uses your existing Identity Provider (IDP) to authenticate users with administrative access. The general flow is that you open `/login` in a browser, select the IDP you want to use, and then are redirected to the IDP login page. After successful login, you are redirected back to the Admin API with an auth token in the response cookie (`auth_token`). This token is then used to authenticate all subsequent requests.

Alternatively, you can provide an `authorization: token $API_KEY` header. In order to obtain an API_KEY, talk to your AEM project lead.

tags.name: siteConfig
tags.description:
Please note that most of the configuration objects that have an explicit schema can also be addressed directly, even if not listed in this documentation.
For example the production CDN configuration can be managed via the /config/{org}/sites/{site}/cdn/prod.json endpoint.

tags.name: profileConfig
tags.description:
Please note that most of the configuration objects that have an explicit schema can also be addressed directly, even if not listed in this documentation.
For example the production CDN configuration can be managed via the /config/{org}/profiles/default-profile/cdn/prod.json endpoint.

tags.name: indexConfig
tags.description: A configuration containing one or more index definitions.

tags.name: sitemapConfig
tags.description: A configuration containing one or more sitemap definitions.

get.description: Returns the overall status of the respective resource. (If the `?edit=auto` query param is set, this requires a call to your content source see https://www.aem.live/docs/limits#rate-limits-number-of-preview-operations-per-time)

success.description: Success response of a preview document.

post.description:
Fetches the status of all resources specified in the `paths` property in the payload. If a path ends with `/*`, it is assumed to be a folder and is recursively processed.

The response will contain the folliowing information about a resource: `path`, `sourceLastModified`, `previewLastModified` and `publishLastModified`. It is possible to filter the information returned, by passing a combination of `edit`, `preview` and `live` in the `select` property in the payload.

Note that the bulk status is performed asynchronously and the response will contain information about the created [job](#tag/job).

success.description: Success response.

get.description: Returns the publish status of the respective resource.

success.description: Success response of a published document.

post.description: Publish a resource by copying the resource content from the `preview` content-bus partition to the `live` content-bus partition. It additionally purges the _live_ cdn and the _byo_ cdn, if configured.

success.description: Success response of the published document.

delete.description: Un-publish a resource by deleting the content from from the `live` content-bus partition. It additionally purges the _live_ cdn and the _byo_ cdn, if configured.

post.description:
Updates live resources specified in the `paths` property in the payload.
By default, only new and modified resources are updated, unless the `forceUpdate` property is set to `true`.
Note that configuration files, like `/.helix/config` or `redirects.json`, are always ignored during bulk publish.
Note that the bulk publish is performed asynchronously and this request will return information to the created job.

success.description: Success response.

get.description: Returns the preview status of the respective resource.

success.description: Success response of a preview document.

post.description: Updates the preview resource by fetching the latest content from the content providers (e.g. sharepoint, google docs, BYOM) and storing it in `preview`.

success.description: Success response.

delete.description: Deletes a preview resource from the `preview` partition in the content-bus..

post.description:
Updates preview resources specified in the `paths` property in the payload. If a path ends with `/*`, it is assume to be folder and is recursively previewed.
By default, only new and modified resources are updated, unless the `forceUpdate` property is set to `true`.
Note that configuration files, like `/.helix/config` or `redirects.json`, are always ignored during bulk preview.
Note that the bulk preview is performed asynchronously and this request will return information to the created job.

success.description: Success response.

get.description: Returns the code bus status of the respective resource.

success.description: Success response of a code document.

post.description: Updates the code-bus resource by fetching the latest content from github and storing it in the code-bus. If the last path segment is a `*`, it will recursively update the code-bus with the respective github tree (directory). It additionally purges the _live_ cdn and the _byo_ cdn, if configured.

success.description: Success response.

delete.description: Deletes a code resource from the code-bus It additionally purges the _live_ cdn and the _byo_ cdn, if configured.

post.description: Processes a change event and updates the code-bus accordingly. It additionally purges the _live_ cdn and the _byo_ cdn, if configured.

success.description: Success response.

post.description: Purges the resource from the respective _live_ cdn. It optionally invokes the _byo_ cdn purging hook, if configured.

get.description: Returns the index status of the respective resource.

200.description: The index status of a resource

success.description: Success response of an index document.

404.description: Resource not found or not in index definition.

post.description: Index a resource. If the last path segment is a `*`, it will recursively reindex all published resources below that sub tree.

success.description: Success response of indexing a resource.

404.description: Resource not found or not in index definition.

delete.summary: Remove a resource from the index.
delete.description: Removes a resource from the index.

200.description: Removal of resource in the index triggered.

success.description: Success response of removing a resource from the index.

404.description: Resource not found or not in index definition.

get.description: Provides a selection of available IDP logins

200.description: Display links to IDP specific logins

success.description: Success response.

get.description: Redirects to IDP corresponding the project setup

302.description: Redirects to the IDP authorization endpoint

get.description: Logs out the current session and redirects to the login screen

200.description: Show profile information of logged in user

success.description: Success response.

post.description: Generates a sitemap. The path specified should be the destination path of a sitemap.

success.description: Success response of generating a sitemap

204.description: Path specified is no destination for any sitemap configured

get.description: Returns the job list of jobs

success.description: Success response of a job list.
Success response.
Success response.

get.description: Returns logs for a project. If there are more logs for the timespan specified, there will be a `next` link in the response to load the next batch.

success.description: Success response of a log request.

post.description: Add logs to a project. The body should be in JSON format and contain an array called `entries`.

multiple-entries.description: This posts one entry to the audit log. To see what the added entry would look like, see the second entry in the [getLog](#tag/log/operation/getLogs) example.

get.description: Returns a list of snapshot IDs

success.description: Success response of list snapshots.
Success response of a snapshot manifest.

post.description: Updates the Snapshot manifest and allows to lock or unlock the snapshot. Note that in order to lock the snapshot, the request role also needs to include the `preview:write` permission, and in order to unlock the snapshot, the `live:write` permission is needed.

success.description: Success response of a snapshot manifest.

get.description: Returns the snapshot status of the respective resource.

success.description: Success response of a snapshot document.

post.description: Creates a snapshot of the specified resource and stores it in the preview partition below the `.snapshot/snapshotId` folder. If the path param ends with a `*`, a snapshot of the entire subtree is created. If the snapshot is locked, a `409` response is returned.

delete.description: Deletes a snapshot resource from the `preview` partition in the content-bus.  If the path param ends with a `*`, the entire subtree is deleted. If the snapshot is locked, a `409` response is returned.

post.description:
Add all resources specified in the `paths` property in the payload.
By default, only new and modified resources are updated, unless the `forceUpdate` property is set to `true`.
Note that the bulk snapshot is performed synchronously for arrays with fewer than 200 resources and no wildcard paths, and asynchronously otherwise.
Note that without the appropriate `application/json` content type header, the request will be treated as a wildcard add with no body, resulting in all resources being added to the snapshot.

Publish snapshot resources by copying the resource content from the snapshot location to the `live` content-bus partition. It additionally purges the _live_ cdn and the _byo_ cdn, if configured. If the path param ends with a `*` the resources of the path's entire subtree is published.

success.description: Success response of the published document.
Response of the created publish job.

post.description: Publish a snapshot by copying all resources from the snapshot location to the `live` content-bus partition. It additionally purges the _live_ cdn and the _byo_ cdn, if configured.

success.description: Success response of the published document.
Response of the created publish job.

post.description: Performs a review operation on the snapshot. `request` locks the snapshot; `approve` publishes all resources, clears, and unlocks the snapshot; `reject` unlocks the snapshot. All operations can contain an optional `message` parameter which will be included in logs and events.

503.description: Rate limit / Timeout reached from underlying content source (eg. sharepoint, google docs, BYOM). see https://www.aem.live/docs/limits

post.summary: Create or import org admin API key
post.description: Creates a new admin API key for the org config. If a `jwt` parameter is given, it imports the given key. Please note that the added key can immediately be used and does not need to be added to the `access.admin.apiKeys` property.

Create.description: Create a new key with a given role and a description.

Import.description: Import an existing JWT and annotate with description.

id.description: ID of the admin API key (jti)

value.description: The generated (or imported) admin API key

expiration.description: Key expiration time after which the key will not longer work.

subject.description: the subject of the API key (sub claim)

success.description: Success response of reading an index configuration
success.value:
version: 1
indices:
  default:
    include:
      - '/**'
    target: /query-index.json
    properties:
      lastModified:
        select: none
        value: parseTimestamp(headers["last-modified"], "ddd, DD MMM YYYY hh:mm:ss GMT")

403.description: Access role does not have the required permission

409.description: Conflict. The requested operation is not allowed by the current state of the resource.

500.description: Operation failed due to an internal error.

503.description: Rate limit / Timeout reached from underlying content source (eg. sharepoint, google docs, BYOM). see https://www.aem.live/docs/limits

409.description: Site already has an index configuration, update it instead.

success.description: Success response of reading a sitemap configuration
success.value:
version: 1
sitemaps:
  default:
    source: /sitemap-index.json
    destination: /sitemap.xml
    lastmod: YYYY-MM-DD

403.description: Access role does not have the required permission

409.description: Conflict. The requested operation is not allowed by the current state of the resource.

500.description: Operation failed due to an internal error.

503.description: Rate limit / Timeout reached from underlying content source (eg. sharepoint, google docs, BYOM). see https://www.aem.live/docs/limits

409.description: Site already has a sitemap configuration, update it instead.

changeEntry.description: Describes the changes made to one resource.

path.description: Relative path of changed resource or `*` if this as a branch event.

contentType.description: the content type of the changed resource

indexedUrl.description: URL of document that was indexed. Deprecation Note: The indexed html is always fetched from the preview/live url at the time of publishing and hence not required in helix-index.yaml anymore.

sitemapInfo.description: Information for the sitemap that was generated

created.description: the date and time this configuration was created.

lastModified.description: the date and time this configuration was modified last.

title.description: human readable title. has no influence on the configuration.

description.description: description for clarity. has no influence on the configuration.

^[a-zA-Z0-9-_=]+$.title: Group
^[a-zA-Z0-9-_=]+$.description: A group of members. Can be referenced in access.admin.role.

apikeys.title: ApiKeys
apikeys.description: Stores information about generated admin API keys. The keys listed here have no operational meaning, but are used to track the keys that have been generated.

subject.description: the subject of the API token (sub)

tokens.title: tokens
tokens.description: site tokens. deprecated: use the org secrets instead.

apiKeyId.description: the id of the API key(s) that are allowed to access this org (admin).

roles.description: Admin API roles assigned to the new API key.

description.description: Optional description of the API key' for example to describe its usage.

jwt.description: Optional JWT value for importing an existing API key

id.description: Google drive ID of the root folder; updated automatically when updating the url.

itemId.description: onedrive item ID of the root folder; currently not required as we don't know how setup will work.

content.title: Content source
content.description: Defines the content bus location and source.

overlay.description: Overlay from a BYOM source. Previewing resources will try the overlay source first. Please note, that the overlay config is tied to the base content and not to the site config. I.e. it's not possible to have multiple sites with different overlays on the same base content.

code.title: Code source
code.description: Defines the code bus location and source.

owner.$comment: Note, that owner/repo are no longer part of the hostname and can therefor have more allowed characters than in helix4

route.description: Routes on the CDN that are rendered with Franklin

Routes on the CDN that are rendered with Franklin

host.description: Sidekick config to override the default previewHost. it supports parameters $owner and $repo
Sidekick config to override the default liveHost. it supports parameters $owner and $repo
Sidekick config to override the default reviewHost. it supports parameters $owner and $repo

^[a-z-_]+$.description: The email glob of the users or a group reference for the respective role.

requireAuth.description: Enforce authentication if set to true. If set to 'auto' it will enforce authentication if a role mapping is defined. defaults to 'auto'.

defaultRole.description: the default roles assigned to the users. defaults to `basic_publish` for unauthenticated setups.

apiKeyId.description: the id of the API key(s). this is used to validate the API KEYS and allows to invalidate them.

allow.description: The email glob of the users or a group reference that are allowed access

secretId.description: IDs of the hashed secrets that are allowed.

apiKeyId.description: IDs of the api keys that are allowed. This is deprecated. use `secretId` instead.

badgeVariant.description: The variant of the badge following the Adobe Spectrum badge variants

containerId.description: The ID of the container to add this plugin to

environments.description: The environments to display this plugin in

event.description: The name of a custom event to fire when the button is clicked (defaults to id)

excludePaths.description: Excludes the plugin from these paths

includePaths.description: Includes the plugin on these paths (overrides excludePaths)

isBadge.description: Renders the plugin as a badge

isContainer.description: Renders the plugin as a container for other plugins

isPalette.description: Opens the URL in a palette instead of a new tab

isPopover.description: Opens the URL in a popover instead of a new tab

paletteRect.description: The dimensions and position of the palette (top, left, bottom, right, width, height)

passConfig.description: Append ref, repo, owner, host, and project as query params to the URL

passReferrer.description: Append the referrer URL as a query param to the URL

pinned.description: Renders the plugin in the bar (true, default) or the menu (false)

popoverRect.description: The dimensions of the popover (width, height)

titleI18n.description: The button text in other supported languages

host.description: The host name of the production website (overrides cdn.prod.host)

liveHost.description: The host name of the live environment (overrides cdn.live.host, defaults to *.aem.live)

project.description: The name of the project to display in the sidekick

include.title: Include
include.description: Glob patterns for paths where this index is used.

exclude.title: Exclude
exclude.description: Glob patterns for paths where this index must not be used.

target.description: The data sink to store the extracted data in. This can be an Excel workbook, a Google spreadsheet or an S3 object

properties.title: Properties
properties.description: The properties to add to the index

oneOf.title: Single-Value Property
oneOf.description: The property in an index. The value will be stored as a single cardinal value.

selectFirst.description: A CSS selector expression that selects the first matching node in the HTML (DOM) or Markdown (MDAST) syntax tree

value.description: A ES6 template literal expression that extracts the value from the matching node(s) to be stored in the index

oneOf.title: Multi-value property, concatenated to a single value
oneOf.description: The property in an index. The value will be stored as a single cardinal value.

select.description: A CSS selector expression that selects all matching nodes in the HTML (DOM) or Markdown (MDAST) syntax tree

value.description: A ES6 template literal expression that extracts the value from the matching node(s) to be stored in the index

oneOf.title: Multi-Value Property
oneOf.description: The property in an index. The value will be stored as a list of values

select.description: A CSS selector expression that selects all matching nodes in the HTML (DOM) or Markdown (MDAST) syntax tree

values.description: A ES6 template literal expression that extracts the values from the matching nodes to be stored in the index

hreflang.title: HREF Language
hreflang.description: The HREF language to use when listing alternates according to [RFC 5988](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5988).

hreflang.title: HREF Language
hreflang.description: The HREF language to use when listing alternates according to [RFC 5988](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5988).

source.title: Source
source.description: The source resource path to get records from. These records should at least contain a path property.

origin.title: Origin
origin.description: The origin to prepend to paths found in the source.

destination.title: Path
destination.description: The destination resource path to store sitemap to.

lastmod.title: Lastmod
lastmod.description: The format to use for last modification of a location. If not present, no last modification is added.

languages.title: Sitemap Languages
languages.description: The languages to add to the sitemap

source.title: Source
source.description: The source resource path to get records from. These records should at least contain a path property.

destination.title: Path
destination.description: The destination resource path to store sitemap to.

alternate.title: Alternate
alternate.description: How to compute the respective path in that language. If not present, paths do not contain a language specific part.

extension.title: Location extension
extension.description: Extension to append to every location generated for this sitemap. Default is empty

extension.title: Location extension
extension.description: Extension to append to every location generated for this sitemap. Default is empty

id.description: ID of the admin API key (jti)

expiration.description: Key expiration time after which the key will not longer work.

subject.description: the subject of the API key (sub claim)

statusEditUrlParam.name: editUrl
statusEditUrlParam.description: Optional URL of the edit (authoring) document

forceUpdateRedirectsParam.name: forceUpdateRedirects
forceUpdateRedirectsParam.description: forces an update of the redirects (only applies when updating/publishing redirects.json)

word2mdVersionParam.name: hlx-word2md-version
word2mdVersionParam.description: selects the version to use when invoking the word2md service.

gdocs2mdVersionParam.name: hlx-gdocs2md-version
gdocs2mdVersionParam.description: selects the version to use when invoking the gdocs2md service.

html2mdVersionParam.name: hlx-html2md-version
html2mdVersionParam.description: selects the version to use when invoking the html2md service.

branchParam.name: branch
branchParam.description: Optional branch specifier in case the branch name contains slashes or uppercase characters.

fromParam.name: from
fromParam.description: Indicates the starting date to get logs from. If not specified, uses the current date minus 15 minutes.

toParam.name: to
toParam.description: Indicates the ending date to get logs from. If not specified, uses the current date.

sinceParam.name: since
sinceParam.description: Indicates the time span to retrieve logs for, as a number and a unit in short notation (`s`, `m`, `h` or `d`). Not usable in combination with either `from` or `to`.

nextTokenParam.name: nextToken
nextTokenParam.description: Token returned from a previous call to continue

snFilterParam.name: filter
snFilterParam.description: Filters the resources that are selected for snapshot. If set to `modified` only the resources that have a preview date newer than the live date are selected.

snPublishParam.name: publish
snPublishParam.description: Indicates that the snapshot should be published.

snReviewParam.name: review
snReviewParam.description: State to move the snapshot into.

snReviewMessageParam.name: message
snReviewMessageParam.description: Message of the review action, included in logs and events.

validateParam.name: validate
validateParam.description: indicates to validate the config to be migrated. only effective when `migrate` is set to `true`.

403.description: Access role does not have the required permission

409.description: Conflict. The requested operation is not allowed by the current state of the resource.

429.description: Rate limited by admin service. see https://www.aem.live/docs/limits#admin-api-limits

500.description: Operation failed due to an internal error.

503.description: Rate limit / Timeout reached from underlying content source (eg. sharepoint, google docs, BYOM). see https://www.aem.live/docs/limits
